Title: A PROTEOMICS STUDY OF THE MUNG BEAN EPICOTYL REGULATED BY BRASSINOSTEROIDS UNDER CONDITIONS OF CHILLING STRESS

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes epicotyl elongation and growth recovery under chilling stress
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 CYP90 family; putative BR biosynthetic protein homologous to Arabidopsis CPD/CYP90A1
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CYP90A2 transcript was down-regulated by exogenous BR and up-regulated by the BR biosynthesis inhibitor Brz220; chilling strongly suppressed CYP90A2 transcript and later protein accumulation, supporting a role in BR-associated chilling response and growth regulation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (2-DE plus MALDI-TOF MS/MS); no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Northern blot; Western blot using CYP90A2 antibody; hormone/inhibitor treatment (EBL and Brz220); chilling treatment; sequence homology inference
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper explicitly states CYP90A2 is a putative BR synthetic gene sharing 77% identity with Arabidopsis CPD; authors note further experiments are needed to confirm enzymatic function, so it is treated here as a core common gene rather than a firmly validated pathway enzyme in this study.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript decreased after EBL treatment, increased after Brz220 treatment, and was strongly suppressed by chilling within 1 day; protein level decreased after 3 days of chilling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In epicotyls; suppressed by chilling; feedback-regulated by BR status
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 10°C chilling stress; exogenous 24-epibrassinolide (EBL); Brz220 BR biosynthesis inhibitor
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP90A2 is a mung bean gene highly homologous to Arabidopsis CPD/CYP90A1 and is proposed to participate in BR biosynthesis. 2. Its transcript is feedback-downregulated by EBL and upregulated by Brz220. 3. Chilling strongly suppresses CYP90A2 expression, with protein reduction after prolonged cold. 4. These results support a link between BR homeostasis and recovery of epicotyl growth under chilling.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving chilling tolerance and seedling growth through modulation of BR homeostasis, but direct biochemical confirmation is still needed.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: may contribute to stress protection and protein storage; discussed here in chilling response
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: epicotyl proteins analyzed; named as seed albumin
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Protein abundance was re-up-regulated by BR specifically under chilling conditions, suggesting involvement in chilling response.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (2-DE plus MALDI-TOF MS/MS); no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 2-DE differential protein analysis; MALDI-TOF MS/MS identification
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This protein is not the central mechanistic focus but is one of the core identified proteins discussed as part of BR-mediated chilling recovery.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Re-up-regulated by EBL under chilling, but not under normal conditions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in epicotyl proteome under normal/chilling and EBL treatments
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Seed albumin abundance decreased under chilling and was restored by BR specifically under chilling conditions. 2. Authors infer a role in chilling response rather than normal BR growth signaling.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible marker for BR-associated chilling response, but application value remains preliminary.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Protein abundance was re-up-regulated by EBL after chilling; interpreted as corresponding to BR-mediated cell elongation recovery.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (2-DE plus MALDI-TOF MS/MS); no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 2-DE differential protein analysis; MALDI-TOF MS/MS identification
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a metabolism enzyme or regulator, but a core experimental protein discussed for growth recovery.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Re-up-regulated by EBL under chilling conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Alpha tubulin was suppressed by chilling and restored by EBL. 2. Authors relate this to microtubule organization during BR-mediated epicotyl elongation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primarily mechanistic marker of growth recovery rather than direct engineering target.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified among proteins re-up-regulated by BR under chilling conditions.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (2-DE plus MALDI-TOF MS/MS); no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 2-DE differential protein analysis; MALDI-TOF MS/MS identification
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in the identified protein list but not deeply functionally discussed.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Re-up-regulated by EBL under chilling conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Aspartate-tRNA ligase was one of the proteins restored by BR under chilling. 2. The paper provides limited metabolism-specific interpretation for this protein.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: UDP-sugar and hemicellulose/cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 2-DE differential protein analysis; MALDI-TOF MS/MS identification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Controls biosynthesis of UDP-GlcUA according to paper discussion; key control point suggested
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Re-up-regulated by EBL after chilling, supporting BR-mediated relief of cell wall limitation during epicotyl elongation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein decreased under chilling and restored by EBL
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGD was down-regulated by chilling and re-up-regulated by EBL. 2. Authors infer that BR-mediated growth recovery involves restoration of UDP-GlcUA supply and cell wall biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes UDP-glucose to UDP-glucuronic acid, generating precursor for hemicellulose biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (2-DE plus MALDI-TOF MS/MS); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to support cell wall precursor supply and cold-impaired elongation when combined with BR-related engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports cellular energy demand for elongation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 2-DE differential protein analysis; MALDI-TOF MS/MS identification
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: mitochondrial/chloroplastic F1 ATP synthase catalytic sector
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Re-up-regulated by EBL after chilling, consistent with restored ATP production during BR-mediated growth recovery.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein decreased under chilling and restored by EBL
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F1-ATPase alpha subunit was among proteins restored by BR under chilling. 2. Authors interpret this as part of increased ATP synthesis needed for epicotyl elongation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in ATP synthesis driven by proton motive force as part of F1Fo-ATP synthase complex.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (2-DE plus MALDI-TOF MS/MS); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as an energy-metabolism marker of BR-mediated cold recovery, though not a specific nutrient biofortification target.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: vacuolar proton pumping / cellular energy homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 2-DE differential protein analysis; MALDI-TOF MS/MS identification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Re-up-regulated by EBL after chilling, suggesting involvement in BR-enhanced growth and stress adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein decreased under chilling and restored by EBL
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Vacuolar ATPase abundance was restored by BR under chilling. 2. Authors discuss a possible role in both cell growth and adaptation to stressful conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes ATP to pump protons across endomembrane/vacuolar membranes, acidifying compartments and generating electrochemical gradients.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (2-DE plus MALDI-TOF MS/MS); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stress-adaptation engineering component, though causal nutrient-metabolite engineering value is indirect.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 2-DE differential protein analysis; MALDI-TOF MS/MS identification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme for glycolysis as discussed by authors; not specifically shown rate-limiting here
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Re-up-regulated by EBL after chilling, consistent with recovery of glycolytic ATP production.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein decreased under chilling and restored by EBL
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Enolase abundance dropped under chilling and increased after EBL treatment. 2. Authors connect this change to BR-mediated restoration of ATP-producing metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (2-DE plus MALDI-TOF MS/MS); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker or target for sustaining glycolytic flux under chilling, though broad pleiotropy is likely.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: provides universal methyl donor and metabolic intermediate for growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 2-DE differential protein analysis; MALDI-TOF MS/MS identification; exogenous methionine growth assay as supportive physiological evidence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Up-regulated by EBL under both normal and chilling conditions; implicated in BR-mediated growth recovery and methionine assimilation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein increased after EBL under normal and chilling conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SAM synthetase was re/up-regulated by EBL. 2. Together with methionine synthase induction and methionine-feeding growth promotion, this supports methionine assimilation as a downstream component of BR action.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 10°C chilling stress; exogenous EBL; exogenous methionine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts methionine and ATP to S-adenosyl-L-methionine.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (2-DE plus MALDI-TOF MS/MS); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance methionine/SAM-associated growth resilience under chilling, possibly in combination with BR pathway modulation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential amino acid metabolism and precursor to SAM
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 2-DE differential protein analysis; MALDI-TOF MS/MS identification; exogenous methionine growth assay as supportive physiological evidence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Two methionine synthase protein spots were up-regulated by EBL under both normal and chilling conditions, linking BR action to enhanced methionine assimilation and growth recovery.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Two protein spots increased after EBL under normal and chilling conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two methionine synthase spots were induced by EBL under both temperatures. 2. Exogenous methionine promoted epicotyl elongation similarly to BR. 3. Authors propose methionine assimilation as a downstream branch of BR-mediated chilling recovery.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 10°C chilling stress; exogenous EBL; exogenous methionine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to homocysteine to form methionine.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (2-DE plus MALDI-TOF MS/MS); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising candidate for improving growth under chilling through methionine/SAM metabolism, especially when coordinated with BR signaling.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 2-DE differential protein analysis; MALDI-TOF MS/MS identification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Unlike most other proteins, cytosolic FBP aldolase was down-regulated under chilling and also upon BR treatment, giving an opposite pattern relative to growth recovery.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Authors note contradictory pattern relative to expected ATP demand and suggest further metabolite analysis is needed.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Down-regulated under chilling and upon BR treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FBP aldolase was down-regulated by chilling. 2. It was also down-regulated by BR, unlike most BR-restored proteins. 3. Authors highlight this as an opposing result requiring further study.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reversibly cleaves fructose-1,6-bisphosphate to glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate and dihydroxyacetone phosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (2-DE plus MALDI-TOF MS/MS); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Requires caution due to contradictory regulation; not an immediate engineering target.
